channel.title: Nature
channel.description: Nature is the foremost international weekly scientific journal in the world and is the flagship journal for Nature Portfolio. It publishes the finest peer-reviewed research in all fields of science and technology on the basis of its originality, importance, interdisciplinary interest, timeliness, accessibility, elegance and surprising conclusions. Nature publishes landmark papers, award winning news, leading comment and expert opinion on important, topical scientific news and events that enable readers to share the latest discoveries in science and evolve the discussion amongst the global scientific community.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Author Correction: Broadly stable atmospheric CO<sub>2</sub> and CH<sub>4</sub> levels over the past 3 million years
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 29 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10600-w

Author Correction: Broadly stable atmospheric CO2 and CH4 levels over the past 3 million years
item.dc:title: Author Correction: Broadly stable atmospheric CO<sub>2</sub> and CH<sub>4</sub> levels over the past 3 million years

Score 5/10 C_MAYBE_UNCLASSIFIED

Why: Gemini response missing item; kept to avoid false negative

Abstract: Nature, Published online: 29 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10600-w Author Correction: Broadly stable atmospheric CO 2 and CH 4 levels over the past 3 million years

item.title: Transdimensional anomalous Hall effect in rhombohedral thin graphite
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 29 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10471-1

The experimental observation of a fundamentally new type of anomalous Hall effect that couples both in-plane and out-of-plane orbital magnetizations in multilayer rhombohedral graphene is reported.
item.dc:title: Transdimensional anomalous Hall effect in rhombohedral thin graphite
item.dc:creator: Qingxin Li, Hua Fan, Min Li, et al.; last authors: Jianpeng Liu, Lei Wang
Hua Fan
Min Li

Jianpeng Liu
Lei Wang
item.description:
Authors: Qingxin Li, Hua Fan, Min Li, et al.; last authors: Jianpeng Liu, Lei Wang

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Jianpeng Liu, Lei Wang

Score 9/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Discovery of a new anomalous Hall effect coupling orbital magnetizations in rhombohedral graphene.

Tags: #anomalousHall #graphene #Berrycurvature #topology

Abstract: Nature, Published online: 29 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10471-1 The experimental observation of a fundamentally new type of anomalous Hall effect that couples both in-plane and out-of-plane orbital magnetizations in multilayer rhombohedral graphene is reported.

item.title: Translation-dependent degradation of cas12 mRNA triggered by an anti-CRISPR
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 29 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10440-8

The anti-CRISPR protein AcrVA2 specifically interrupts Cas12a biogenesis by triggering co-translational mRNA degradation.
item.dc:title: Translation-dependent degradation of cas12 mRNA triggered by an anti-CRISPR
item.dc:creator: Nicole D. Marino, Alexander Talaie, Milan Gerovac, et al.; last authors: Jörg Vogel, Joseph Bondy-Denomy

Abstract: Nature, Published online: 29 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10440-8 The anti-CRISPR protein AcrVA2 specifically interrupts Cas12a biogenesis by triggering co-translational mRNA degradation.

item.title: Demography and life histories across the Roman frontier in Germany 400–700 ce
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 29 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10437-3

Analysis of 258 ancient genomes from southern Germany reveals a major demographic shift during the late fifth century, yet family structures persisted from Late Roman times, demonstrating cultural continuity despite profound demographic reorganization.
item.dc:title: Demography and life histories across the Roman frontier in Germany 400–700 ce
item.dc:creator: Jens Blöcher, Leonardo Vallini, Maren Velte, et al.; last authors: Daniel Wegmann, Joachim Burger

Abstract: Nature, Published online: 29 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10437-3 Analysis of 258 ancient genomes from southern Germany reveals a major demographic shift during the late fifth century, yet family structures persisted from Late Roman times, demonstrating cultural continuity despite profound demographic reorganization.

item.title: Intrinsic polar vortex crystals in A-site layer-ordered perovskites
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 29 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10470-2

A two-dimensional polar hedgehog lattice spontaneously forms in layer-ordered perovskites, showing stable topological ferroelectric states without external constraints and enabling new pathways for functional material design.
item.dc:title: Intrinsic polar vortex crystals in A-site layer-ordered perovskites
item.dc:creator: Chao Xu, Nengneng Luo, Junyi Yue, et al.; last authors: Xiaoqing Pan, Ye Zhu
Nengneng Luo
Junyi Yue
Changsheng Chen
Tieyuan Bian
Chi Zhang
Xiangli Che
Jianwen Liang
Molly Meng-Jung Li
Jun Yin
Zhen Chen
Shujun Zhang
Xiaoqing Pan
Ye Zhu
item.description:
Authors: Chao Xu, Nengneng Luo, Junyi Yue, et al.; last authors: Xiaoqing Pan, Ye Zhu

Last/corresponding-author proxy: Xiaoqing Pan, Ye Zhu

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Spontaneous topological ferroelectric hedgehog lattice in perovskites; relevant for topological phase discovery.

Tags: #topological #perovskites #polarvortex

Abstract: Nature, Published online: 29 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10470-2 A two-dimensional polar hedgehog lattice spontaneously forms in layer-ordered perovskites, showing stable topological ferroelectric states without external constraints and enabling new pathways for functional material design.

item.title: #ScientistAtWork 2026: Nature seeks striking photographs that capture researchers at work
item.content:encoded:
Nature, Published online: 27 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01266-5

Competition winners receive a cash prize and will see their images featured in Nature.
item.dc:title: #ScientistAtWork 2026: Nature seeks striking photographs that capture researchers at work
item.dc:creator: Jack Leeming, Alexia Austin
Alexia Austin

Abstract: Nature, Published online: 27 April 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01266-5 Competition winners receive a cash prize and will see their images featured in Nature.
